source_title: REGENETEN Advanced Healing Solutions - clinical evidence collection
text: with open-architecture HEALICOIL◊ PK Anchors (n=36) or non-vented TWINFIX◊ HA Anchors (n=33) Conclusion Open-architecture HEALICOIL PK Anchors significantly improved bone ingrowth compared to non-vented TWINFIX HA Anchors 6 months after rotator cuff repair. Collection of Evidence Next study →Previous studies → Overview Results • Significantly more HEALICOIL PK Anchors demonstrated good or excellent bone ingrowth compared to TWINFIX HA Anchors (69.5 vs 36.3%; p<0.001; Figure) • No significant differences between HEALICOIL PK Anchor and TWINFIX HA Anchor groups in rates of cyst formation (14 vs 12%) and re-tear (5 vs 5%) • Significant improvements in shoulder function and pain relief from baseline with both anchors (p<0.001); no significant differences between groups Partial-thickness tears A comparison of open-construct PEEK suture anchor and non-vented biocomposite suture anchor in arthroscopic rotator cuff repair: a prospective randomized clinical trial18 Kim JH, Kim YS, Park I, et al. Arthroscopy. 2020;36:389–396. 2.8% 27.8% 38.9% 30.6% 24.2% 39.4% 3.0% 33.3% Figure. Bone ingrowth (modiﬁed Barber’s ossiﬁcation scale) with HEALICOIL PK Anchors and TWINFIX HA Anchors on 6-month CT scans Percentage of patients HEALICOIL PK (n=36) Poor Fair Good Excellent TWINFIX HA (n=33) 0 20 40 60 80 100 FT+PT tears Smith+Nephew ADVANCED HEALING SOLUTIONS Biomechanical study using 3D-FEM virtual pull-out testing to compare fixation properties, stress distribution and failure patterns in screw-type (TWINFIX◊ Ti Suture Anchor) and open architecture (HEALICOIL◊ PK Suture Anchor and HEALICOIL REGENESORB◊ Suture Anchor) suture anchors. Computer models of each anchor were inserted into a model of cancellous bone prior to simulation of a traction force Collection of Evidence Next study →Previous studies → Overview • Site of highest stress distribution and element failure differed by anchor type: – Around the proximal threads of the screw-type TWINFIX Ti Anchor – Near the distal tip and site of suture thread attachment in both coil-type HEALICOIL Anchors • HEALICOIL REGENESORB Anchors showed the least displacement of the three anchors (Figure) Partial-thickness tears Comparison of fixation properties between coil-type and screw-type anchors for rotator cuff repair: a virtual pullout testing using 3-dimensional finite element method19 Sano H, Tokunaga M, Noguchi M, et al. J Orthop Sci. 2016;21:452–457. Results Pre-clinical Figure. Mean value of displacement for open architecture and screw-type suture anchors 1000 200 300 400 500 0 0.05 0.10 0.15 0.20 Displacement (mm) Tensile load (N) TWINFIX Ti Suture Anchor 5.0mm HEALICOIL PK Suture Anchor 4.5mm HEALICOIL REGENESORB Anchor 4.75mm Conclusion Failure of the screw-type TWINFIX Ti Anchor occurred closer to the bone surface than with the open architecture HEALICOIL Anchors in virtual pull-out testing. As proximal bony tissue is often damaged during repair, this may indicate a greater risk of pull-out with screw-type anchors. Smith+Nephew ADVANCED HEALING SOLUTIONS Collection of Evidence Osteointegration of a biocomposite suture anchor after arthroscopic shoulder labral repair20 Sugaya H, Suzuki K, Yoshimura H, et al. Arthroscopy. 2019;35:3173–3178. Clinical trial evaluating osteoconductivity, tissue density of anchor sites, shoulder function and adverse events at 2 years post-arthroscopic labral repair with 148 REGENESORB◊ Anchors* implanted in 37 shoulders with traumatic instability (n=37 patients) Overview Results • Ossification was observed in 89.9% (133/148) of anchor sites, with no significant difference between 2.3mm and 2.9mm anchors, or between anchor sites (Figure) • Mean tissue density of anchor sites was significantly greater than infraspinatus muscle (p<0.001) but significantly lower than cancellous bone (p<0.001) • Significant improvements in shoulder function scores were observed at 2 years post-operatively compared to pre-operative levels: – JSS-SIS: 96.3±4.7 versus 63.1±13.9 points (p<0.001) – Rowe score: 96.6±4.6 versus 39.9±18.0 points (p<0.001) • No adverse events or anchor failures were observed Conclusion REGENESORB suture anchors used for arthroscopic labral repair demonstrated high levels of osteoconductivity and led to significant improvements in shoulder function scores compared to pre-operative values. NA Figure. OQS scores two years post-arthroscopic labral repair Due to rounding, the proportions of anchors in each type category do not exactly sum to total values for osteoconductivity/no osteoconductivity. †Types 2, 3 and 4 are considered to display osteoconductivity according to previous studies Osteoconductivity No osteoconductivity 89.9% of REGENESORB Anchors showed osteoconductivity 10.1% 89.9% Type 1 (little/no ossiﬁcation): 10.1% Type 2 (partial ossiﬁcation): 64.2%† Type 3 (ossiﬁcation with a thin lucent rim): 23.6%† Type 4 (good ossiﬁcation): 2.0% † Ossiﬁcation quality score *Labral repair anchors used in this study were non-commercialised REGENESORB anchors in an OSTEORAPTOR◊ shape. Smith+Nephew ADVANCED HEALING SOLUTIONS Osteoconductive resorption characteristics of a novel biocomposite suture anchor material in rotator cuff repair21 Vonhoegen J, John D, Hägermann C. J Orthop Surg Res. 2019;14:12. Retrospective study evaluating implant resorption, osteolysis, re-tear rates and pull-out complications in 48 patients receiving 82 HEALICOIL◊ REGENESORB◊ Suture Anchors at a mean follow-up of 21 months after rotator cuff repair • At 21 months, 79% (65/82) of anchors could not be distinguished from adjacent bone material on MRI (Figure) • Osteolysis was detected in only 2.4% (2/82)
